body_text:
23:22 â September 29, 2023
FX Notes Plus â FedEx Shipment Liability Update
The Exchange Bank of Canada (EBC) adjusted their policies to align with FedEx liability limits due to a change in domestic insurance guidelines. We updated procedures for foreign currency shipments to reflect the new liability limit:
Maximum liability of $10,000 CAD equivalent per FedEx Envelope or Box; up to four envelopes or boxes per delivery address, per day.
Amounts that exceed this limit will be delivered by armoured courier, for which a fee is charged.
We also updated shipping instructions to indicate the requirement to package currency in dual custody under HD cameras.
Currency Volume
FX Notes Plus
Entire Part
We replaced all âsecure siteâ references with âClient Centreâ and revised the included definitions.
Section 7.1, Overview of Selling Foreign Currency and Drafts
We added a note that reflects the liability limit.
Section 7.3, Foreign Currency and Draft Order Deadlines and Delivery Methods
We added a note that reflects the liability limit.
Section 13.1, Overview of Shipping Foreign Currency
We added a note indicating that you must package currency under HD cameras. Any claims made will require video footage.
Section 13.2, Processing Foreign Currency for Shipment
We added that you must leave a noticeable signature with a permanent marker along the sealed portion of the envelope; and you must ensure that the courier scans each package being picked up.
